steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The prediction was that positive feedback would produce two effects: psychological (enhanced enjoyment) and physical (improved endurance). The question asks for a finding where positive feedback worked psychologically but not physically — greater enjoyment but similar, not improved, exercise duration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — positive feedback group reported greater enjoyment but similar exercise duration: directly shows the dissociation — psychological benefit without physical benefit. ✓
  • Choice B — focuses on a different feedback group: does not isolate the specific effect of positive feedback on both dimensions. ✗
  • Choice C — obscures positive feedback's specific effects: does not clearly separate psychological and physical outcomes for the positive feedback group. ✗
  • Choice D — suggests positive feedback improved physical endurance: contradicts the required dissociation — the physical prediction would be confirmed, not weakened. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
